text:
Key pillars:
- **Tracking-first**: structured rich inputs (fields, counters, selectors), never raw Markdown typing.
- **Rich inputs → Markdown output**: Markdown is the storage/portability layer, not the UI. [...] Logging a set is one or two taps — the weight/reps pre-fill from the plan or your last session, you nudge them if reality differed. A set is either "as planned" (one tap) or "adjusted" (tap the number, dial it). Skipping, substituting, adding a set, flagging pain/fatigue are all one gesture away, never buried in a menu. The distinction between *prescribed* (ghost) and *actual* (solid) is always visible.
